title: Add tlsVerify bool to SearchImage for varlink
body:
Cockpit wants to be able to search images on systems without
tlsverify turned on.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body: Fixes https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/2293
this needs a `make varlink_generate_api_generate` to update the api documentation; after that, LGTM

When the registry containing the image we are searching for is listed in insecure registries section in conf file and ```tlsVerify: true``` is passed to the API, there is not error but the image is returned.
Is this expected?

According to the cli docs for tlsVerify this should not happen, https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/docs/podman-search.1.md

If you manually set the flsVerify flag to true and the registry is listed as insecure.  Then the containers/image should only use the registry if it can be verified.  IE it will connect with https only not fall back to http://.  At least that is my understanding.  @umohnani8 @vrothberg @mtrmac Please verify.

> If you manually set the flsVerify flag to true and the registry is listed as insecure. Then the containers/image should only use the registry if it can be verified. IE it will connect with https only not fall back to http://. At least that is my understanding. @umohnani8 @vrothberg @mtrmac Please verify.

Correct. This behaviour is expected according to the docs and also the podman CLI follows this behaviour.
However, the varlink API does not respect the tlsVerify parameter if the registry is listed in insecure section, but instead it always returns the images from insecure registries.
@KKoukiou You are saying with this patch varlink is working differently then podman search --tlsverify=true?

@rhatdan Correct. 
```
$ sudo varlink call unix:/run/podman/io.podman/io.podman.SearchImage '{"name": "my-ubuntu", "tlsVerify":true}'
{
  "images": [
    {
      "description": "",
      "is_automated": false,
      "is_official": false,
      "name": "my-ubuntu",
      "star_count": 0
    }
  ]
}


$ sudo podman search my-ubuntu --tls-verify=true
ERRO[0000] error searching registry "localhost:5000": couldn't search registry "localhost:5000": pinging docker registry returned: Get https://localhost:5000/v2/: http: server gave HTTP response to HTTPS client 
```
my ubuntu image exists in a local private registry, and the configuration looks like this.
```
$ sudo cat /etc/containers/registries.conf 
[registries.search]
registries = ['localhost:5000']

[registries.insecure]
registries = ['localhost:5000']
```
Ok then that is a bug.

I think we have an issue with the Varlink API, in that we have three settings.

One Use Default TLSVerify (Whatever is set in registries.conf)
Force on, force off.

In the CLI we can check if the user specified a tlsverify flag and then set it, otherwise we use default.

In the API, how should we indicate this. Perhaps have three contants.  Default, On, Off?
There was talk of an OptionalBool type (unset, true, false) elsewhere
Yes we are using that, but I don't see how we do that in Varlink.
@baude Does varlink have optional parameters?

> @baude Does varlink have optional parameters?

not per'se ...
@baude A nullable bool aka "?bool" should work, yes?  Use nil for Default and then true or false if set by user.
you saw this in varlink docs as valid ?
@baude Correct. Search https://varlink.org/Interface-Definition for nullable.
@jwhonce @baude I have made tlsVerify optionalBool,  Not sure how you call this from Varlink, but I think this allows the passing of the optionalBool.
@jwhonce @baude PTAL
LGTM, lets give it a try!
@mtrmac It is fine with me to change the semantics, @baude @jwhonce WDYT?

The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
LGTM with respect to the API.  This will break the API tests in python-podman.
Yes @jwhonce, can you give me a /lgtm
/lgtm
